intro: Glenniea adami is a plant species native to Africa, belonging to the family Burseraceae and is commonly known as Adam's tree. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The bioactive constituents of Glenniea adami include sesquiterpenes, diterpenes, and other triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Glenniea adami is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions including fever, cough, and skin infections.
